name: plotly
description: Interactive visualization library. Use when you need hover info, zoom, pan, or web-embeddable charts. Best for dashboards, exploratory analysis, and presentations. For static publication figures use matplotlib or scientific-visualization.
capabilities: Generate code for 40+ chart types including scatter, line, bar, histogram, heatmap, 3D plots, and geographic maps
Create interactive visualizations with hover tooltips, zoom, pan, and click events for data exploration
Customize chart layouts, colors, themes, and styling for professional presentations and publications
Export charts to HTML, PNG, PDF, and SVG formats for web embedding and static reports
Build complex multi-trace figures and subplots for comparative analysis
Integrate Plotly with pandas DataFrames for seamless data pipeline visualization
author: K-Dense Inc.
securitySummary: This is a legitimate documentation skill for the Plotly visualization library. All 342 static findings are false positives from the pattern scanner detecting markdown code block delimiters as shell commands, documentation text as malicious keywords, and legitimate API references as threats. No actual security risks present.
valueStatement: Data visualization often requires complex code and lacks interactivity for exploration. This skill provides comprehensive guidance for creating interactive, publication-quality charts with Plotly, featuring hover tooltips, zoom, pan, and 40+ chart types for dashboards and data analysis.
limitations: Requires Plotly library installation which may not be available in all environments
Interactive features only work in environments that support HTML rendering or Jupyter notebooks
Large datasets may require performance optimization or data aggregation before visualization
Static export formats require additional dependencies like kaleido or orca

useCases.targetUser: Data analysts and business intelligence developers
useCases.title: Dashboard Development
useCases.description: Build interactive dashboards for business intelligence with real-time data exploration capabilities including filtering, zooming, and hover details.

useCases.title: Scientific Data Visualization
useCases.description: Create publication-quality charts for research papers, presentations, and reports with precise control over styling and layout.

useCases.title: Exploratory Data Analysis
useCases.description: Quickly visualize data distributions, correlations, and patterns with interactive charts for hypothesis generation and data quality assessment.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Chart Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a scatter plot showing the relationship between temperature and sales using Plotly Express

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Series Line Chart
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate an interactive line chart comparing monthly revenue across three product categories with hover tooltips
promptTemplates.scenario: Time series comparison for business reporting

promptTemplates.title: Custom Styled Visualization
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a histogram with custom colors, bin sizes, and annotations showing data distribution with mean and median lines

promptTemplates.title: Advanced Multi-Plot Layout
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a dashboard with subplots containing a scatter plot, bar chart, and heatmap arranged in a grid layout with shared color scales

bestPractices: Use Plotly Express for quick standard visualizations and Graph Objects for fine-grained control over complex custom charts
Enable responsive sizing with fig.update_layout for charts that adapt to different screen sizes and container dimensions
Optimize large datasets by aggregating data or using sampling techniques before visualization to maintain interactivity performance
antiPatterns: Avoid using Graph Objects for simple charts when Plotly Express can achieve the same result with less code
Do not create overly complex charts with too many traces or data points that make interaction slow or confusing
Never hardcode chart dimensions without considering responsive design for different display environments

faq.question: When should I use Plotly Express versus Graph Objects?
faq.answer: Use Plotly Express for quick standard visualizations with pandas DataFrames. Use Graph Objects when you need precise control over individual components or chart types not available in Plotly Express.

faq.question: How do I make my charts work in Jupyter notebooks?
faq.answer: Plotly charts display automatically in Jupyter notebooks when you call fig.show(). Ensure you have the notebook renderer installed with pip install notebook.

faq.question: Can I export Plotly charts to static images?
faq.answer: Yes, install kaleido with pip install kaleido, then use fig.write_image for PNG, PDF, or SVG export. HTML export works without additional dependencies.

faq.question: How do I customize chart colors and themes?
faq.answer: Use the color parameter in Plotly Express or update_traces for individual series. Apply built-in themes with fig.update_layout or create custom color scales.

faq.question: What is the difference between show and write methods?
faq.answer: The show method displays charts interactively in supported environments. Write methods like write_html and write_image save charts to files for sharing or embedding.

faq.question: Does this skill require internet connection?
faq.answer: No, Plotly works offline. Charts can be exported with embedded JavaScript libraries for offline viewing without external CDN dependencies.
